name: C C M H
description_short: CCMH Federal Credit Union is a member-owned nonprofit serving healthcare workers and their families in West Virginia with savings accounts, checking, and personal loans.
description_long:
CCMH Federal Credit Union (Camden Clark Memorial Hospital FCU) was established to serve the financial needs of employees and families associated with Camden Clark Memorial Hospital in Parkersburg, West Virginia, along with staff from affiliated medical practices and organizations throughout West Virginia and Ohio. The credit union operates as a non-profit institution with NCUA insurance protection, maintaining a lean operational model with four employees managing over 1,200 member accounts.

The credit union offers regular share savings accounts, share draft checking accounts, and a variety of personal loans. Members can access online banking, mobile banking via phone, debit cards, check reordering, and ATM services. They currently promote a spring loan special of $10,000 at 8% APR over 60 months. Share draft checking requires no minimum balance and includes dividend payments, while regular savings accounts require a $5 minimum balance maintained at all times. Membership requires a one-time $1 fee and $5 opening deposit.

CCMH FCU distinguishes itself through a highly restricted field of membership focused on healthcare professionals and their dependents, creating a tight-knit member community. The credit union maintains conservative operations with over $5 million in assets and $3.1 million in outstanding loans. Their limited hours of operation (weekday mornings and one extended Thursday) reflect their small-scale, specialized mission rather than broad consumer banking.

This is a niche credit union best suited for eligible employees of Camden Clark Memorial Hospital and affiliated medical practices. The restrictive membership requirements, minimal branch presence, limited hours, and small asset base mean members have fewer services compared to larger regional credit unions. However, members benefit from personalized service, non-profit structure, and rates competitive for their market segment.
services: Regular share savings accounts with quarterly dividend compounding and $5 minimum balance
Share draft checking accounts with no minimum balance requirement and monthly dividends
Personal secured loans available immediately upon membership
Visa debit/check card for point-of-sale transactions and ATM access
Online banking with account access and statement management
Free ATM access at unspecified location(s)
pros: Non-profit NCUA-insured credit union structure eliminates profit motive in lending and savings
No minimum balance requirement for share draft checking accounts, only NSF fees of $32
Competitive spring loan special: $10,000 at 8% APR for 60 months (~$205/month)
Monthly dividend compounding on share draft checking with rates set by member-elected board
Quarterly dividend compounding on regular savings accounts
Low membership barriers: $1 one-time fee and $5 minimum opening deposit
Free online and mobile banking access with no monthly account maintenance fees
cons: Highly restrictive membership limited to Camden Clark Memorial Hospital employees, affiliated medical practices, and immediate family—most consumers cannot join
Very limited hours: closes at 2:30 p.m. Monday-Wednesday and Friday, only open until 4:30 p.m. Thursday
Signature loans require one-year membership tenure; only secured loans available immediately
Minimal staff (4 employees) limits service availability and may create longer wait times
$2 fee charged for mailed statements rather than free online statements
best_for: Camden Clark Memorial Hospital employees and medical staff in Parkersburg, WV
Employees of affiliated medical practices listed in membership criteria (Ernest Miller Jr., D.O.; Fred Jay Krieg, Ph.D.; F.G. Powderly, M.D., etc.)
Spouses and immediate family members of eligible employees seeking member-owned banking
Employees of ServCare Home Health Agency, AMIS Systems Inc., and Medical Financial Consultants at approved locations
diagnosis: CCMH FCU is exclusively for healthcare workers and families affiliated with Camden Clark Memorial Hospital and partner medical organizations in West Virginia—not for general consumers. The restrictive membership, minimal hours, and small staff make it impractical for anyone outside the eligible field, but valuable for qualifying members seeking non-profit banking with personalized service and board-determined rates.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: C C M H — Credit Union in Parkersburg, WV | CreditDoc
meta_description: C C M H — NCUA-insured credit union in Parkersburg, WV. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
